title: Pembric II
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{CelestialBody |image=[[File:Pembric_II.jpg]] |name=Pembric II |region=[[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} |sector=[[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]]''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' |system=[[Pembric system/Legends|Pembric system]]{{DarkStryder|adventure=Crisis (adventure)|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}} |coordinates=M-21 |xyz= |distance= |suns= |orbited= |position= |moons= |routes= |lengthday=33 [[Standard hour/Legends|standard hours]] |lengthyear=302 local [[Standard day/Legends|days]] |class=Terrestrial |diameter= |atmosphere=[[Atmosphere/Legends|Type I]] {{C|breathable}} |climate=*[[Temperature/Legends|Temperate]] *Warm |gravity=[[Gravity/Legends|Standard]] |terrain=*[[Beach/Legends|Beaches]] *[[City/Legends|Urban]] *[[Volcano/Legends|Volcanic fields]] *Wetlands |water= |interest=*[[Erwithat Spaceport]]''[[Vision of the Future]]'' *[[ThrusterBurn Tapcafe]] |flora= |fauna= |otherlife= |species= |otherspecies=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |socialgroup= |language=*[[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] *[[Jarellian]] |government=*[[Pembric II corporate government|Corporate government]] *[[Pembric II government|Puppet government]] under the control of the [[Bombaasa Cartel]] |population=200 million |demonym= |cities=[[Erwithat (city)|Erwithat]] |imports=*[[Foodstuff/Legends|Food]] *Technology *Weapons |exports=*[[Smuggler/Legends|Contraband]] *[[Hfredium]] [[Ore/Legends|ore]] *[[Gemstone/Legends|Precious stones]] |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] *[[Moff Sarne's forces]] *Bombaasa Cartel }} {{Quote|What a desolate hole. No wonder the Empire never really policed the region; who would want the bloody place?|Captain Kaiya Adriemtrum's private thoughts upon entering the Pembric system|}} '''Pembric II''', also referred to as '''Pembric 2''', was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Pembric system/Legends|Pembric system]], located in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Although it was originally [[Terraforming/Legends|terraformed]] as an [[Farm/Legends|agricultural]] colony, careless actions during the process resulted in unsuitable conditions, creating boggy wetlands and a near-constant light [[Water/Legends|rain]] to fall. Situated in a remote part of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], and partially isolated by the presence of a dense [[Pembric asteroid field|asteroid field]] and [[Radiation/Legends|radiation]] fields, Pembric II was marketed by its residents as a resort for [[Gangster/Legends|gangsters]], which eventually evolved into a full-blown [[Shadowport/Legends|shadowport]]. Soon, a cultured [[Scoundrel/Legends|criminal]] element based themselves off Pembric II, including the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Crev Bombaasa/Legends|Crev Bombaasa]], who controlled a large [[Bombaasa Cartel|cartel]]. The [[Pembric II corporate government|government]] of Pembric II turned a blind [[Eye/Legends|eye]] to the organized crime due to the large influx of [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] they contributed to the planetary economy. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Pembric II came under the control of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], and later [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]]. Both parties utilized Pembric II as a source of raw materials, processing [[hfredium]] [[Ore/Legends|ore]] [[Mining/Legends|mined]] from the [[Star system/Legends|star system's]] [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid field]] on the planet for shipment to Imperial-allied arms corporations. In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], Moff Sarne's control of the [[Sector/Legends|sector]] was broken when [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[New Republic task force (Kal'Shebbol)|forces]] [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|evicted]] him from his [[Capital/Legends|capital]] on [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]]. Bombaasa moved quickly, taking control of the planet by establishing a [[Pembric II government|puppet government]] with pro-Imperial leanings in the hope of dissuading the New Republic from interfering in Pembric II's affairs. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], the New Republic [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' [[DarkStryder Campaign|arrived]] at Pembric II in [[Mission to Pembric II|search of]] [[Technician/Legends|technical experts]] to assist them in solving problems that they were experiencing with their [[Starship/Legends|ship]]. They ran afoul of Bombaasa and his new regime, but managed to rescue an imprisoned [[Slicer/Legends|slicer]] named [[Gaelin]] to assist them. ==Description== {{Quote|I hope you weren't planning on sneaking in, by the way. The way your people are lighting up the field, all of Pembric 2 knows we're coming by now.''"
"''Actually, according to Mara, most ships have to do some blasting on the way in. Even the locals who supposedly know the routes in and out.|Shada D'ukal and Talon Karrde|}} [[File:Kathol.jpg|thumb|left|A map of the Kathol sector]] Pembric II, also styled as Pembric 2, was located in the [[Pembric system/Legends|Pembric system]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]' [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] at grid coordinates M-21. It was connected via [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] to four locations: the [[Galtea system/Legends|Galtea]], [[Sebiris system/Legends|Sebiris]], and [[Shintel system/Legends|Shintel systems]], and the [[Nebula/Legends|nebulae]] containing the [[Tanquilla Beach/Legends|''Tanquilla Beach'']] [[Shadowport/Legends|shadowport]]. Utilizing a Class One [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], travel to [[Galtea/Legends|Galtea]] took a [[Standard day/Legends|day]] and eight [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]], transit to [[Sebiris]] was two days and fourteen hours long, [[Shintel]] was three days distant, and it took nineteen hours to reach the ''Tanquilla Beach'' [[Space station/Legends|space station]]. A dense [[Pembric asteroid field|asteroid field]] in the [[Star system/Legends|system]], coupled with erratic [[Radiation/Legends|radiation]] fields, gave Pembric II a measure of isolation from the rest of the [[Sector/Legends|sector]]. A terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|world]], Pembric II had a temperate but warm climate, and a drizzling [[Water/Legends|rain]] fell constantly. The [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] was breathable for most [[Species/Legends|species]], with standard [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]], and was dominated by boggy wetlands and [[City/Legends|urban settlements]] such as [[Erwithat (city)|Erwithat]]. The planet also boasted a large amount of [[Beach/Legends|beaches]] and a field of [[Volcano/Legends|volcanoes]]. Days on Pembric II lasted for thirty-three standard hours, and a [[Standard year/Legends|year]] was comprised of 302 local days. The population numbered 200 million, and was mostly [[Human/Legends|Human]]. Although Pembric II was nominally controlled by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], and later [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]], for part of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]—a conflict fought between the Empire and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]—a [[Pembric II government|puppet government]] was installed in [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]] by [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Crev Bombaasa/Legends|Crev Bombaasa]] to take control of the planet. Local [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|law enforcement]] was undertaken by the [[Pembric Security Legion]], but the organization was corrupt and controlled by Bombaasa's [[Bombaasa Cartel|Cartel]]. Due to its relative isolation, the planet became a haven for [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]], [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]], and other [[Scoundrel/Legends|criminal elements]], evolving into a shadowport.''[[Pirates & Privateers]]'' Items defined as contraband by both the Empire and the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] were major exports, along with [[hfredium]] [[Ore/Legends|ore]] and other [[Gemstone/Legends|precious stones]]. [[Foodstuff/Legends|Food]], technology and weapons were always in demand for importation. ==History== ===Gangster's paradise=== Pembric II was originally a barren rock of a planet, scarred by multiple [[Meteorite/Legends|meteorite]] impacts. It was [[Terraforming/Legends|terraformed]] with the intention of establishing an [[Farm/Legends|agricultural]] colony, but the process suffered from careless procedures. The resulting planet was habitable, but uncomfortable and unsuited for its intended purpose. A constant drizzle and boggy wetlands prevented mass agriculture, and the construction of the city of Erwithat and its [[Erwithat Spaceport|spaceport]] was only achieved after a difficult feat of [[Engineer/Legends|engineering]]. The planet became home to rough, frontier settlers and criminals. Despite its failure as an agricultural colony, the population made the most of Pembric II's natural assets, which included scenic beaches and volcanic fields. Due to Pembric II's isolated location in the Outer Rim Territories, a true tourist industry never flourished. However, canny individuals marketed the planet to certain clientèle who found other tourist destinations to be too legally restrictive for business opportunities. In short, Pembric II became a resort world for [[Gangster/Legends|gangsters]]. As gangsters set up their operations on the planet, [[Cantina/Legends|cantinas]], [[Museum/Legends|museums]] and entertainment venues were constructed to service them. That in turn attracted more orderly and cultured criminals who soon overshadowed the planet's original population. [[Pembric II corporate government|Pembric II's corporate government]] was fully aware of the situation and turned a blind [[Eye/Legends|eye]] to the smuggling and organized crime being conducted as long as [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] continued to flow into the economy. The Pembric Security [[Legion/Legends|Legion]], the planetary police force, gained a reputation for corruption. ===Imperial rule=== [[File:CrevBombaasa.jpg|thumb|right|Crev Bombaasa, crime lord]] When Imperial Moff Kentor Sarne was appointed to rule the Kathol sector in [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], he utilized the Pembric system as a [[Mining/Legends|mining]] outpost. Hfredium ore culled from the system's [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid field]] was processed on Pembric II and shipped to various Imperial-allied arms manufacturers. Many new workers were transported to the planet to toil for the Empire in the processing plants. The Imperial presence caused a downturn in criminal activity on the planet, and Pembric II's beachfront residences were affected by the rise in industrial activity. Sarne controlled the planet for a number of years, both in an official capacity as a representative of the Empire and, after the [[Death/Legends|death]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] and the subsequent fracturing of [[Imperial High Command/Legends|Imperial High Command's]] authority,{{DarkStryder|adventure=The Saga Begins|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}} as an independent warlord. During that [[Time/Legends|time]], the Moff allowed the planet to remain a haven for the criminal element, seeing the advantage in having connections to the underworld—rumors stated that he had an uneasy alliance with the Bombaasa Cartel, run by the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] crime lord Crev Bombaasa. The Moff used Pembric II as a training ground for his [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] and intelligence operatives, staging infrequent crackdowns on the population. In 8 ABY, Moff Sarne was [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|ousted]] from his [[Capital/Legends|capital]], [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]], by the New Republic and his authority over planets in the region was broken. Crev Bombaasa moved quickly to secure the planet for himself, taking control of the mining operations in the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] field and purging the Imperial loyalists on the planet—although some remained by keeping their loyalties a secret. With word spreading throughout the sector that the New Republic had taken Kal'Shebbol and was spreading its influence throughout the region, Bombaasa installed a puppet government. The new body claimed to be pro-Imperial, which Bombaasa hoped would be enough to stave off New Republic interest in the system. The population was ready for a change in government after Imperial rule, and was willing to accept and support the New Republic. The workers brought to the planet by the Empire were stranded by the collapse of Imperial authority in the region, and with the Cartel's takeover, much of the populace wanted to leave. The government attempted to dissuade them by instituting expensive "[[transit visa]]s" and "[[exit pass]]es" that were required for off-world departure. An underground business soon emerged that took advantage of the situation, selling transport off-world for extortionate prices. A [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]], [[Kebbo]], served as [[Chief Magistrate (Pembric Security Legion)|Chief Magistrate]] of the Security Legion. Kebbo was in Bombaasa's employ, and the organization soon came to enforce the Cartel's wishes while, on the surface, maintaining local law and order. Any being opposing Bombaasa could not count on help from the Legion, and they regularly put down civilian protests with violence. ===The New Republic arrives=== {{Quote|Well, that's decided that, gang. Somewhere on Pembric II is a tech crew with our name on it. Yes, Loh'khar? What can I do for you?''"
"''Ah, Captain, the question is, what can I do for ''you''? I have, perhaps, shown some slight ability to locate desired items, and desired people as well, in the past.''"
"''And, of course, you know exactly where to locate the people we need?''"
"''Most assuredly, Captain. Our good Master Spang at the ''Beach'' suggested starting with a fellow named Drake at one of the spaceport taverns, did he not? I require no further leads.''"
"''I know I'm going to regret this, but carry on. Get me what we need. I don't like sitting on a time-bomb, Finder.|Captain Kaiya Adrimetrum and Loh'khar the Finder|}} [[File:FarStar.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The New Republic CR90 corvette ''FarStar'']] The same year, the New Republic [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' [[DarkStryder Campaign|traveled]] to Pembric II seeking a [[Gender/Legends|man]] named [[Breslin Drake]], who was the manager of the [[ThrusterBurn Tapcafe]] in Erwithat. The ''FarStar'' had been plagued with technical difficulties caused by several [[Programmer/Legends|programming]] trapdoors and booby traps installed on the [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] by its former owner, Moff Sarne. After one such system was triggered in the Shintel system, destroying the hyperdrive, the ''FarStar'' was forced to make repairs at the ''Tanquilla Beach'' shadowport. During the refit, the engineer [[Spang]] noted that the programming reminded him of work he had seen done by [[Slicer/Legends|slicers]] operating from Pembric II. He directed [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Kaiya Adrimetrum]], [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of the ''FarStar'', to Drake, who he believed would be able to lead them to an individual capable of assisting them in removing all the hidden systems.{{DarkStryder|adventure=Shintel Downtime|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}} Aware of the planet's reputation, and reasoning that an armed New Republic vessel would not be welcome, Adrimetrum decided to [[Mission to Pembric II|send a team]] led by procurement specialist [[Loh'khar]] the [[Quartermaster/Legends|Finder]] to the planet posing as illegal [[Arms dealer/Legends|arms merchants]]. They would use the ''FarStar''{{'s}} [[Unidentified Aegis-class combat shuttle|''Aegis''-class combat shuttle]], stripped of New Republic markings, as their [[Transport/Legends|transport]]. However, one of Bombaasa's mining outposts in the asteroid field detected the [[Corvette/Legends|corvette's]] arrival and relayed the information to the crime lord. After landing at the [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]], the delegation was met by [[Chief/Legends|Chief]] Magistrate Kebbo of the Pembric Security Legion, who had been made aware of their allegiance but had been ordered not to reveal that knowledge. After the New Republic team paid the appropriate bribes to ensure the safety of their [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] while on-world, Kebbo directed them to Drake and tasked two [[Pembric Security Legionnaires|Legionnaires]] to escort them to the [[Tapcafe/Legends|tapcafe]]. However, the two Legionnaires were secretly deep-cover [[Agent/Legends|agents]] loyal to Moff Sarne, and they organized an ambush which the New Republic delegation was able to survive. In the aftermath, the team found a [[Dataplaque/Legends|data plaque]] containing transit visas. Upon reaching the ThrusterBurn, they made contact with Drake who told them that the [[Keiffler Brothers]]—whom he regarded as the best slicers in the sector—had been contracted to work for the Empire on Kal'Shebbol but had not returned. One of their apprentices, [[Gaelin]], had returned, claiming that the brothers had been [[Death/Legends|killed]] by the Empire. He had eventually set himself up in business, but was soon absorbed into Bombaasa's operations. Gaelin wanted to leave the planet, but Bombaasa kept him locked away, working in secret. During the conversation, Kebbo arrived with a [[Squad/Legends|squad]] of Legionnaires and informed the ''FarStar'' delegation that Bombaasa wished to see them in the [[Casino/Legends|casino]] at the rear of the tapcafe. Bombaasa stated that he knew of their allegiance, the presence of the ''FarStar'' in the asteroid field, and that Pembric II was pro-Imperial. While he did not care what business had brought them to his planet, he did seek reassurance that the ''FarStar'' was not the vanguard of a New Republic assault and wanted to make a deal to keep the New Republic out of the Pembric system. However, he doubted that the [[mission group]] had the authority to sign such a treaty, so directed them to leave as soon as possible, as long as they could afford the tariffs to depart the planet. Upon the mention of transit visas and exit passes, Kebbo realized that the guards he had assigned to escort the crewmembers were missing and that they had been carrying the needed documentation. As Kebbo prepared to search the delegation, they resisted, leading to a confrontation. The New Republic delegation was assisted by Drake and four of his [[Bouncer (occupation)/Legends|bouncers]], who helped them in escaping the tapcafe and took them to a small warehouse on the outskirts of the spaceport to lay low. {{RPGseed}} Drake, who had been looking for a way to leave the planet himself, struck a deal with the ''FarStar'' crew: he would lead them to Gaelin so they could extract him in return for passage off-world for him and his bouncers. At sunset, Drake led the delegation to another [[Bombaasa Cartel storage depot|warehouse]] on the edge of the spaceport, noting that Bombaasa had hidden Gaelin there instead of the [[tech district]] to avoid being obvious. As a result, security was not exceptionally tight so as to avoid suspicion, but neither would it be easy to infiltrate. Two teams were formed—one to infiltrate the warehouse and secure Gaelin, and another to retrieve their shuttle and fly it to the warehouse roof for extraction. The warehouse team successfully subdued the Cartel guards with the help of a score of local laborers and convinced Gaelin of their peaceful intentions. The other team liberated their shuttle and flew it to the warehouse. The shuttle extracted the rest of the mission group, Drake and his bouncers, Gaelin, and the laborers who had assisted at the warehouse. {{Endgame}} ===Karrde games=== [[File:KarrdeNJO.jpg|thumb|right|Talon Karrde]] {{Quote|It's not too late to scrub this. We can go back to the ship, get out of here, and take our chances with whatever Bombaasa decides to throw at us.''"
"''We've been objects of official curiosity ever since we landed. If we try to leave now, Bombaasa's people will intercept us.|Shada D'ukal and Talon Karrde|}} Around [[13 ABY/Legends|13 ABY]], independent businessbeings [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]] and [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] arrived on Pembric II in search of an individual named [[Jorj Car'das]], a former smuggler. They were working on behalf of smuggler chief [[Talon Karrde]], who had previously worked for Car'das. While on the planet, the pair foiled an [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]] attempt targeting Bombaasa; when the crime lord offered a reward, Jade refused. She said that she was working for a "chieftain," and Bombaasa agreed to defer repayment of the debt until it was needed. Six years later, in [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]], Karrde visited Pembric II himself while attempting to contact Car'das in the hopes of attaining a copy of the [[Caamas Document]] to end a potential [[Caamas Document Crisis|crisis]] threatening to plunge the New Republic into civil war. Arriving aboard his [[Action IV transport]] ''[[Wild Karrde]]'', Karrde and his associate, the [[Mistryl Shadow Guard]] [[Shada D'ukal]], attempted to meet with Bombaasa at the ThrusterBurn Tapcafe. They were accosted by a [[Unidentified swoop gang (Pembric II)|swoop gang]] outside the establishment, but D'ukal, with Karrde's assistance, dealt with the threat. Ushered in to see Bombaasa by members of the Security Legion, Karrde traded on Mara Jade's earlier debt to secure safe passage through [[Realspace/Legends|space]] controlled by the Bombaasa Cartel by acquiring an [[ID overlay]] that identified the ''Wild Karrde'' as a friendly ship to Bombaasa's allied vessels. ==Inhabitants== {{Dialogue|Erwithat Space Control|Bss'dum'shun. Sg'hur hur Erwithat roz'bd bun's'unk. Rs'zud huc'dms'hus u burfu.|Shada D'ukal|I thought you said they spoke Basic here.|Talon Karrde|They do. They must be trying to throw us. Threepio? Do you recognize it?|C-3PO|Oh, yes, Captain Karrde. I am fluent in over six million forms of communication. This is the dominant Jarellian dialect, a language whose antecedents date back to–|D'ukal|What did he say?|C-3PO|He has identified himself as Erwithat Space Control, Mistress Shada, and asks our identity and cargo.|attr=Erwithat Space Control queries the ''Wild Karrde'' on approach to Pembric II|src=}} The 200-million-strong population of Pembric II was mainly Human, but representatives of other races, such as Rodians, were present. Residents mainly spoke [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]], but some spoke [[Jarellian]]. A percent of the population was not native to Pembric II, having been transported to the planet by the Empire during its reign to work in the hfredium ore processing plants. ==Locations== {{Quote|Take us into orbit. There's only one place on the planet that can handle a ship this size, the Erwithat Spaceport. They should be calling with landing instructions anytime now.|Talon Karrde|}} The main city on Pembric II was named Erwithat, which housed the main spaceport. Construction of the city and the port were achieved shortly after the planet was settled, and its establishment proved challenging for the settlers due to unfavorable building conditions. The spaceport had several landing pads and warehouses within its limits. Erwithat was also the location of the ThrusterBurn Tapcafe, which was owned by Crev Bombaasa. Closely situated to the spaceport, it was frequented by [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] and criminals, and included a casino in the rear from which Bombaasa did business. Erwithat also contained a tech district within its limits. ==Behind the scenes== Pembric II first appeared in [[Crisis (adventure)|''Crisis'']], the fifth [[Roleplaying game|RPG adventure]] featured in ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' published by [[West End Games]] in [[1995]]. It was written by [[Eric S. Trautmann]] and [[Paul Sudlow]], with illustrations by [[Tim Bobko]]. Pembric II received a mention in [[1996]]'s ''[[The Kathol Outback]]'', the second book of the [[The DarkStryder Campaign (series)|DarkStryder series]], as well ''[[Pirates & Privateers]]'', published in [[1997]]. Pembric II—styled as Pembric 2—was later featured in [[1998]]'s ''[[Vision of the Future]]'', a novel by [[Timothy Zahn]], and received an entry in [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. During the events of ''Crisis'', players may attempt to flee from or negotiate with the Imperial agents posing as Pembric Security Legionnaires. Neither option will work, as the agents will engage the characters in combat regardless. When formulating a plan to spring Gaelin, the gamemaster can use Drake to suggest splitting the team to cover both objectives—securing Gaelin and retrieving the shuttle—if the players do not think of it. The adventure assumes that both objectives are implemented simultaneously, and this article reflects that assumption. Retrieving the shuttle and flying to the warehouse holding Gaelin will tip the Cartel off to the character's intent if implemented before raiding the warehouse, at which point more Legionnaires are dispatched to oppose the players. After the successful extraction, the gamemaster has the option of sending [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wing starfighters]] operated by the Pembric Security Legion after the escaping shuttle. ==Appearances== *{{DarkStryder|adventure=Shintel Downtime|book=The DarkStryder Campaign|reprint=yes}} {{1stm}} *{{DarkStryder|adventure=Crisis (adventure)|book=The DarkStryder Campaign|reprint=yes}} {{1st}} *{{DarkStryder|adventure=Traitor in our Midst|book=The DarkStryder Campaign|reprint=yes}} {{Mo}} *''[[Vision of the Future]]'' {{Ab|Vision of the Future (unabridged audiobook)}} ==Sources== *''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' *''[[The Kathol Outback]]'' *''[[Pirates & Privateers]]'' *''[[The DarkStryder Campaign, Deluxe]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Galactic Empire planets]] [[Category:Kathol sector locations]] [[Category:Moff Sarne's planets]] [[Category:Planets in the Outer Rim Territories]] [[Category:Shadowports]] [[Category:Terrestrial planets]] [[Category:Trade worlds]] [[Category:Type I atmosphere planets]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Pembric II locations]] }}
